items.title: Work-based learning funding raised to RM9.9mil, recognising armed forces personnel experience
items.summary: MELAKA: Allocation for the Work-Based Learning (WBL) programme for the armed forces has been increased from RM3 million to RM9.9 million this year, expanding opportunities for 1,520 personnel to obtain academic recognition based on their experience and skills gained throughout their service. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the strategic initiative is aimed at improving the employability of armed forces personnel after service, while helping them secure more stable jobs and income. "So far, a total of 461 armed forces personnel have benefited from the WBL programme, with 231 enrolled at Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) and 230 at Universiti Pertahanan Nasional Malaysia (UPNM), and all are expected to graduate in November and December this year.

items.title: Iris issued UMA query as stock surges 66.7pct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia has issued an unusual market activity (UMA) query to Iris Group Bhd following a sharp rise in the company's share price. At the time of writing, Iris Group's shares had surged 66.67 per cent, or 12 sen, to 30 sen, making it the second most active counter on Bursa Malaysia. The stock opened at 18.5 sen, up from its previous close of 18 sen, and climbed to an intraday high of 33 sen. A total of 59.78 million shares changed hands, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM244.71 million.

items.title: Jobless rate at decade low, youth unemployment remains high
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The national unemployment rate remained at its lowest level in a decade in March, but young people are still facing significant challenges in securing jobs, particularly those newly entering the labour market. MBSB Research, BIMB Securities Sdn Bhd and TA Securities confirmed that the country's overall unemployment rate remained stable at 2.9 per cent. MBSB Research said the persistently high youth unemployment rate signals that the transition process for graduates and new jobseekers into the labour market is still not running smoothly.

items.title: On national Youth Day, Anwar announces RM5m allocation for Youth Council
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced a RM5 million allocation for the Malaysian Youth Council (MBM) to strengthen the umbrella body’s role in advancing youth development in the country. He said the additional RM1 million allocation compared with last year was recognition of MBM’s role as the main driver of the national youth agenda under the Ministry of Youth and Sports (KBS), while reflecting the government’s continued commitment to supporting youth development programmes. Also present were Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun and Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari.

items.title: Suspected mastermind in nude assault, robbery remanded, hunt on for two more
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: An unemployed man believed to be the mastermind behind the robbery and assault of a woman who was allegedly forced to strip naked and recorded on video was remanded for six days by the magistrate's court here today. Magistrate Nurasidah A. Rahman issued the remand order against the 32-year-old suspect and another man, 46, until Wednesday to facilitate investigations. Police also recovered a Glock 17 pistol from the vehicle.

items.title: DAP leaders urged to end infighting, uphold unity
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: DAP veteran and former Penang executive councillor Datuk Seri Phee Boon Poh has called on party leaders to uphold stability, discipline and collective responsibility amid ongoing internal tensions involving the Penang state government. Phee said Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow had shown "considerable patience, restraint and professionalism" in leading the state government despite repeated internal provocations over the years. Phee said party discipline and collective responsibility must always be upheld, especially among senior leaders with extensive experience in government and party affairs.

items.title: New MACC chief Halim Aman vows stronger cooperation with AGC on high-profile cases
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 15 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will continue strengthening its strategic cooperation with the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC), particularly in handling high-profile, large-scale and public interest cases. Newly-appointed MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said such cases would continue to be referred to the AGC as the authority responsible for determining legal action on investigations conducted by the anti-graft agency. Meanwhile, Mohd Dusuki expressed appreciation for Abd Halim’s decision to make the AGC his first courtesy visit as the new MACC chief commissioner.

items.title: Azmin appointed as Bersatu election director
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has agreed to appoint former Selangor menteri besar Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali as the party's election director ahead of possible state polls. Azmin, who is also Bersatu secretary-general, said the decision was made at the party's supreme council meeting yesterday in place of recent developments in Negri Sembilan and Melaka, which are expected to face state elections in the near future. He also said the supreme council has agreed to appoint Bersatu Armada chief Muhammad Hilman Idham as the party's representative in the PN supreme council.

items.title: Jho Low pardon bid: Anwar says ‘we are not going that route’, leaves US decision to Washington
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia will not consider any pardon application involving fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low. Asked whether the government would formally oppose any move by the United States (US) regarding the matter, Anwar said: “As far as we are concerned, we are not going that route...but of course, what the US decides is their own business.” The Prime Minister also said that he was unaware of any pardon application submitted by Jho Low. According to the report, approval of the application would result in the US criminal charges against Jho Low being dropped.

items.title: Interest-driven travel trends open new opportunities for Southeast Asian countries
items.summary: This shift is expected to benefit regional tourism markets which have been actively positioning themselves as a hub for ecotourism, wellness tourism, food tourism, luxury hospitality and cultural experiences amid the broader recovery in international travel. Industry participants at the recent Xiaohongshu Business x Singapore Outbound Tourism Forum noted that tourism demand is increasingly being shaped by lifestyle interests, cultural experiences, wellness activities, sporting events and local immersion rather than traditional destination-driven travel. Analysts said the trend could create significant opportunities for Malaysia's tourism sector, particularly as travellers increasingly seek authentic cultural experiences, premium wellness offerings, nature-based tourism and experiential urban travel across Southeast Asia.

items.title: MN Holdings bags RM83.5mil data centre infrastructure contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MN Holdings Bhd has secured an RM83.5 million contract to build a new 275 kilovolt (kV) consumer landing station (CLS) for a data centre in the southern region of Peninsular Malaysia. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said its wholly-owned subsidiary MN Utilities Engineering Sdn Bhd accepted the letter of award on May 15 from a customer involved in infrastructure hosting, data processing services and related activities. MN Holdings said the contract is expected to contribute positively to the group's future earnings and net assets per share upon commencement of the project.

items.title: Cops bust international scam syndicate, seize RM57.68mil in assets, arrest 187
items.summary: Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said Op Teguh 2.0, from May 6-7, involved 46 raids on homes, including luxury condominiums in the Klang Valley. "Police seized syndicate assets worth RM57.68 million, including three luxury properties valued at RM38 million. Khalid said police also confiscated 67 luxury items, including watches, handbags, jewellery, wallets and gold bars worth RM12 million.

items.title: Loke puzzled by opposition to UEC pathway into public universities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said he was puzzled by opposition from certain quarters to the recognition of the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) for admission into public institutions of higher learning. He said the move would expand access to education. It was previously reported that Gabungan Pelajar Melayu Semenanjung (GPMS) Perak had opposed the Higher Education Ministry's recognition of the UEC.

items.title: Bank Negara: Inflation outlook hinges on global energy and commodity price movements
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's inflation outlook will continue to depend largely on external cost developments, particularly fluctuations in global energy and commodity prices. Headline inflation rose to 1.6 per cent during Q1 2026, in part reflecting the initial passthrough of higher global cost pressures. Abdul Rasheed said the surge in global energy prices led to higher domestic fuel prices in March, namely for RON97 and unsubsidised diesel, contributing to the increase in headline inflation.

items.title: [UPDATED] No corruption found in KL flood retention pond land probe – MACC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has found no evidence of corruption in the change of land status involving several Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) flood retention ponds. MACC senior investigations director Datuk Mohd Hafaz Nazar said the case had been classified as "No Further Action" (NFA) following investigations into alleged abuse of power involving several DBKL and Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) officers between 2015 and 2021. "In 2022, the MACC opened an investigation paper following complaints regarding allegations of abuse of power involving several officers from Kuala Lumpur City Hall and the Kuala Lumpur Department of Irrigation and Drainage for the period between 2015 and 2021.

items.title: Anwar: Jho Low’s US pardon application still subject to court process
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Malaysia will not oppose a bid by a fugitive businessman involved in the massive 1MDB corruption scandal to seek a pardon from US President Donald Trump, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Speaking to reporters during a visit to Seremban district, south of Kuala Lumpur, Anwar described the plea by Low Taek Jho as a “non-issue”. The businessman, better known as Jho Low, is formally seeking a “pardon after completion of sentence”, according to the US Department of Justice website.

items.title: Anwar increases Malaysian Youth Council allocation to RM5mil
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced a RM5 million allocation for the Malaysian Youth Council (MYC) to strengthen the umbrella body's role in advancing youth development nationwide. He said the increase of RM1 million from last year's allocation reflected the government's continued commitment to supporting youth development programmes and recognised MBM's role in driving the national youth agenda under the Youth and Sports Ministry. "MBM is the main youth body under the ministry.

items.title: Fiery crash involving trailer, van and car at Udini underpass leaves Penang traffic at standstill
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 15 — Three vehicles caught fire following a five-vehicle crash at the Udini Underpass on Jalan Masjid Negeri here today, causing severe traffic congestion. Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations John Sagun Francis said there were no reports of casualties. “Upon arrival, we found that the accident involved five vehicles, three of which were on fire.

items.title: Anthony Loke labels backlash against UEC public university access an attempt to politicise education
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke today slammed criticism over the move to open public universities to graduates of Chinese Independent High Schools (SMPC) and Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) holders, describing it as an attempt to politicise education. Yesterday, the Higher Education Ministry (KPT) announced that students from outside the national education system, including UEC holders, would be allowed to enrol in public universities, marking a watershed decision as the UEC system has long been a flashpoint in race relations. He claimed that combining tahfiz graduates and UEC holders under the same announcement was a subtle attempt to legitimise the admission of UEC students into public universities.

items.title: Court grants more time for mental evaluation of Sabah murder accused
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The magistrate's court here today granted a one-month extension for the psychiatric evaluation of a septuagenarian charged with murdering a woman whose dismembered body parts were found in Indah Permai, Sepanggar. The court fixed June 25 for further mention pending completion of the mental evaluation report from Hospital Mesra Bukit Padang. Piluta was not brought to court during today's proceedings.

items.title: Teacher bags RM1mil BSN SSP jackpot ahead of Teachers' Day
items.summary: IPOH: A secondary school History teacher from Butterworth has turned this year's Teachers' Day into a life-changing moment after winning RM1 million in the Bank Simpanan Nasional (BSN) Sijil Simpanan Premium (SSP) monthly millionaire draw. Despite becoming a millionaire overnight, Munisuaran said he remains committed to his long-standing savings discipline. "I treat SSP as a proper savings plan.

items.title: Pahang to meet ministry next week over river sand exports
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Pahang government will meet the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry next week to discuss issues related to river sand exports in greater detail. He said the federal government's restrictions on river sand export permits had directly affected sand industry operations in Pahang, particularly in downstream and estuary areas that require regular dredging. A day later, Wan Rosdy said the state was seeking federal approval to resume river sand exports to strengthen state revenue.

items.title: AirAsia X's stock drops amid Q1 loss
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AirAsia X Bhd is the most actively traded stock on Bursa Malaysia after it logged losses of RM154.9 million in the first quarter. Additionally, analysts lowered their earnings forecasts and target prices for AirAsia X. Meanwhile, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) lowered its FY26 earnings forecast to RM331 million from RM1.4 billion, and FY27 estimates to RM1 billion from RM1.4 billion, factoring in lower capacity and higher jet fuel costs.

items.title: Tiger teeth, bear fangs among RM5.32m wildlife parts seized in Kota Kinabalu raid
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 15 — Sabah wildlife authorities believe they have uncovered an illegal wildlife parts trading operation in the heart of the city following a raid on an antique shop along Jalan Gaya here two days ago. In a statement today, Sabah Wildlife Department director Soffian Abu Bakar said the raid uncovered several wildlife parts from protected species, including 16 tusks believed to be from wild boars, nine teeth believed to be from tigers, 10 teeth suspected to be from bears, various edible bird’s nest products of different weights, as well as 13 stones believed to be from porcupines. The case is being investigated under Sections 41(1) and 41(2) of the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997 for allegedly possessing, storing and displaying wildlife parts without valid permits.

items.title: RM57m in luxury houses, handbags, watches, cars seized as police crack international scam ring based in Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Police have arrested 187 suspects from nine countries in a major crackdown on international online scam syndicates operating out of the Klang Valley. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Khalid Ismail said the operation, dubbed Op Teguh 2.0, involved 46 raids on residential premises believed to be used as bases for cross-border scam activities. Khalid said the groups used several scam tactics, including fake investment platforms targeting victims in Hong Kong and China, impersonation scams involving fake Japanese police officers on Telegram, romance scams aimed at victims in Korea and Japan, and illegal online gambling operations.

items.title: A-GC and MACC 'inseparable', says new chief commissioner Abdul Halim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Newly appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman paid Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar a courtesy visit yesterday, marking his first official engagement since taking the helm. Also present during the meeting were A-GC Trial and Appeals Division chief Datuk Seri Saiful Edris Zainuddin and Prosecution Division chief Datuk Masri Mohd Daud. Abdul Halim took over as chief commissioner on May 13, succeeding Tan Sri Azam Baki, who ended his tenure on May 12 after leading the commission since 2020.

items.title: Gobind: Malaysia must go beyond investments to become AI powerhouse, attract global firms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's ambition to become a regional artificial intelligence (AI) powerhouse cannot be achieved through investment attraction alone but requires a fundamental redesign of how government and industry operate, said Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo. Gobind's remarks come as Malaysia continues to position itself as a key digital investment destination, having secured RM144.4 billion (US$30 billion) in data centre and cloud investments between 2021 and June 2025. The broader digital investment pipeline has surpassed RM232.8 billion (US$59 billion) as of April 2025.

items.title: No need for panic buying, rice supply stable until year-end – Mat Sabu
items.summary: KLANG: There is no need for panic buying as the country's rice supply is sufficient and expected to remain stable until the end of the year, says Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu. Purchase as normal," he said. Mohamad warned that panic buying or excessive purchases would only disrupt supply chains and create unnecessary shortages.

items.title: Woman loses RM245,000 to fake govt tender scam
items.summary: MIRI: A businesswoman lost RM245,500 after being duped by a fake government tender for the supply of electronic goods. Miri police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the 61-year-old victim was approached by a suspect posing as a government agency officer. The victim, who is in the construction industry, was offered a tender to source electronic items and was given a second phone number to arrange the procurement.

items.title: Maxis posts higher earnings in Q1 on service revenue growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Maxis Bhd's net profit rose 12.4 per cent to RM417 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM371 million a year ago, supported by steady service revenue growth. By segment, service revenue grew 3.5 per cent year-on-year to RM1.57 billion, driven by customer growth and tailored offerings across different segments. Consumer home subscriptions rose 2.5 per cent to 807,000, although service revenue edged down 1.2 per cent to RM251 million amid intensified price competition.

items.title: Anwar: No fuel rationing needed
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Malaysia's fuel supply remains sufficient despite global tensions, and there is currently no need for rationing or restrictions on vehicle use, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He also said Malaysia's position remains stable compared with some neighbouring countries, where fuel stations have reportedly been forced to close amid tightening supply conditions. However, the prime minister, who is also finance minister, also said that Malaysia remains in a comfortable position in terms of fuel supply.

items.title: Strong fundamentals, reforms to support ringgit; up 3.3pct versus US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's firm economic prospects and sustained reform momentum are expected to provide lasting support for the ringgit moving forward, said Bank Negara Malaysia. Bank Negara governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said, despite some volatility following the Middle East conflict and reduced expectations for US Federal Reserve policy rate cuts, the ringgit has performed well on a year-to-date basis. "Looking ahead, while external factors will continue to drive exchange rate movements, Malaysia's firm economic prospects and sustained reform momentum are expected to provide enduring support to the ringgit.

items.title: Westports earnings rise 47pct in Q1 to RM326mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Port operator Westports Holdings Bhd posted stronger earnings for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, driven by higher container revenue. Revenue increased 44.2 per cent to RM896.28 million from RM621.29 million previously. The group's operational revenue came in at RM753 million, up 27 per cent year-on-year, mainly supported by higher container-related revenue.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: MACC yet to give update on report over shrinking KL retention ponds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh is awaiting a response from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) over alleged irregularities tied to the alienation of the Batu and Jinjang flood retention ponds. The minister was responding to comments by former Federal Territories Minister Datuk Seri Tengku Adnan Tengku Mansoor , who denied any knowledge of flood retention pond land transfers to developers during his tenure, claiming land matters were outside his ministry's purview at the time. Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud confirmed that guidelines have been tightened.

items.title: Drugs, phone, weapon found inside Ipoh police lock-up, Bukit Aman orders full investigation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail has ordered a full investigation after drugs and other prohibited items were found inside a police lock-up in Ipoh, Perak. I have instructed district police chiefs to inspect lock-ups from time to time. “All state police chiefs have the authority to conduct inspections, and district police chiefs also carry out the same duties.

items.title: Workplaces not safe if employees face burnout, harrassment: Lee Lam Thye
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Workplaces cannot be considered safe if employees are suffering from burnout due to unrealistic workloads or are subjected to bullying and harassment, according to Mental Health Advisory Council member Lee Lam Thye. "Just as we recognise the importance of physical health in ensuring that employees are able to perform their duties safely and effectively, we must now acknowledge that mental health is equally - if not more - critical in shaping productivity, decision-making, relationships, and overall organisational success," he said in a statement today. He said that mental health also underpins physical health, with chronic stress closely linked to serious conditions such as heart disease, hypertension, weakened immunity and sleep disorders.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia lower at midday, tracking regional peers amid US-China Summit watch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended the morning trading session lower, tracking weaker regional market performance as investors remained cautious and kept a close watch on developments from the US-China Summit. At 12.30 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 6.11 points, or 0.35 per cent, to 1,739.47 from Thursday's close of 1,745.58. Turnover stood at 2.36 billion units valued at RM1.65 billion.

items.title: Teachers brave lake journeys to educate Orang Asli children at floating school
items.summary: For Syed Nafiz Muqri Danial Syed Ahmad Ezahar, 27, and Muhammad Aizuddin Asmadi Afandi, 26, serving at the country's first Tasik Banding floating school is not merely about teaching in a classroom, but ensuring Orang Asli children are not left behind in education. They are among teachers from Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) RPS Kemar K11 who take turns teaching on the floating boats from Monday to Wednesday. Ahead of Teachers' Day celebrations, Syed Nafiz, from Sungai Petani, Kedah, said the experience had changed his perspective about the teaching profession.

items.title: CGC joins Bank Negara's Stabilisation Relief Facility to help SMEs amid Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Credit Guarantee Corp Malaysia Bhd (CGC) has participated in Bank Negara Malaysia's effort to assist small and medium enterprises (SMEs) experiencing cash flow pressures amid the ongoing economic uncertainty through the SME Stabilisation Relief Facility. It will be made available through participating financial institutions. CGC Malaysia chairman Datuk Mohammed Hussein said CGC is privileged to play a central role in assisting the SMEs.

items.title: IGP confirms probe into claims fugitive Jho Low attended secret meetings in KL in 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Investigations are ongoing into allegations that fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, attended secret meetings in Kuala Lumpur last year, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. He said the investigations are strictly in accordance with existing legal procedures. “Therefore, investigations are carried out from time to time in accordance with the legal process permitted under the law,” he told reporters here.

items.title: Sarawak minister says 253 minors charged, four fined over smoking and vaping offences in first five months of 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Four minors were issued compounds for the illegal purchase of smoking products between January and May this year, said Sarawak Women, Childhood and Community Wellbeing Development Minister Datuk Seri Fatimah Abdullah. She said the offence was committed under Section 13(3) of the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024. During the same period, she said 253 minors were charged under Section 17 (1) for smoking or vaping-related offences, while three vendors were charged for selling smoking products to minors under Section 13(1) of the same act.

items.title: Sarawak court sends 19-year-old to Henry Gurney School until 21 over child sexual abuse content
items.summary: KUCHING, May 15 — The Sessions Court here has ordered a 19-year-old student to be sent to Henry Gurney School Kuching until he reaches the age of 21 for having child sexual abuse materials on his smartphone. Judge Musli Ab Hamid also ordered the youth offender to undergo rehabilitative counselling throughout his detention period at Henry Gurney School Kuching in accordance with Section 26(1) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act (Soaca) 2017. The court further ordered the offender to undergo police supervision in Bau for two years upon his release from the institution pursuant to Section 27(1) of Soaca 2017.

items.title: Sabah Law Society backs state govt’s delay to federal border agency rollout over autonomy concerns
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 15 — The Sabah Law Society (SLS) has expressed support for the state government’s decision to defer the implementation of the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS), describing the move as necessary to safeguard Sabah’s constitutional rights and immigration autonomy. Its president Datuk Mohamed Nazim Maduarin said the State Cabinet’s decision demonstrated “legal foresight” by prioritising constitutional certainty over administrative expediency, particularly in matters involving Sabah’s special safeguards under the Federal Constitution and the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63). Nazim said the SLS concurred with the state government’s assessment that the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency Act 2024 (Act 860), in its current form, posed significant legal complexities in relation to Sabah’s autonomy.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh says MACC report on shrinking KL retention ponds lodged in 2021
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department Hannah Yeoh today revealed that a report over Kuala Lumpur’s shrinking flood retention ponds had already been lodged with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in 2021. “The report was made back in 2021 when the massive floods hit Kuala Lumpur. Three days ago, Yeoh ordered a halt to all development approvals involving land surrounding the Jinjang and Batu flood retention ponds after revealing the sites had shrunk to just 30 per cent of their original size.

items.title: With vape-related drug cases rising in Sarawak, minister says full ban still on the table
items.summary: KUCHING, May 15 — The Sarawak Government does not rule out imposing a complete ban on vaping in future, said Dato Sri Fatimah Abdullah. “The Sarawak Government believes that public awareness, prevention, and community readiness must first be strengthened to ensure that any future policy, including a possible complete ban, can be implemented effectively and receive wider public support,” she said. “Enforcement actions are being carried out in close collaboration with the Royal Malaysia Police, through the Sarawak Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department, together with the Sarawak State Health Department.

items.title: Terengganu civil servant duped of almost RM100,000 in Telegram investment scams
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 15 — A female civil servant in Terengganu lost RM99,276 after falling victim to two fraudulent online investment schemes. Kuala Terengganu district police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the 38-year-old victim responded to a ‘’Webull investment scheme’’ advertisement on the Telegram application on May 1, which promised lucrative returns within just six hours. “The deceived victim made 12 payments totalling RM48,965 into four different accounts between May 1 and 7,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms in northern peninsula, parts of Selangor, Johor and Sarawak until noon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to prevail until noon today in six states, according to the Meteorological Department. It said in a statement that the adverse weather will affect Perlis, Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Yan, Pendang and Kuala Muda) as well as Penang. Similar conditions are expected in Kuala Selangor and Klang in Selangor; Batu Pahat, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru in Johor and Sibu, Mukah, Bintulu (Tatau and Bintulu) and Miri (Subis) in Sarawak.

items.title: Rat droppings found at Penang school canteen, 14-day closure order issued
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 15 — The canteen of a school here has been ordered to close after a health inspection found rat droppings on the premises. Penang Education director Mohd Dziauddin Mat Saad said the closure order took immediate effect for fourteen days from noon yesterday until May 27. “Investigations and monitoring at the canteen found rat droppings on the floor behind an unused refrigerator, and a closure order for unsanitary food premises under Section 11 of the Food Act 1983 was issued,” he told Bernama when contacted today.

items.title: Fishermen recover another body from sunken Indonesian migrant boat off Pulau Pangkor, death toll now 10
items.summary: IPOH, May 15 — Another body from the boat carrying Indonesian migrants that sank off Pulau Pangkor was recovered last night, bringing the death toll to 10. The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) said in a statement that the man’s body was found by fishermen at about 7.40pm yesterday, around 3.6 nautical miles northeast of the location where the boat sank. In an incident on May 11, a boat believed to be carrying 37 Indonesian migrants sank about 8.2 nautical miles off Pulau Pangkor while travelling to several destinations in Malaysia.

items.title: Anwar should oppose Jho Low pardon bid with direct letter to Trump, says Guan Eng
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim should immediately write to US President Donald Trump to formally oppose fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho’s alleged pardon application linked to the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal, Lim Guan Eng said today. Low, better known as Jho Low, was charged in 2018 but remains at large. According to Lim, officials from the US Department of Justice had also alleged that US$1 billion in kickbacks was distributed among participants in the scheme, with Jho Low allegedly pocketing at least US$1.42 billion.

items.title: Malaysia backs dialogue, diplomacy to resolve West Asia conflicts at BRICS meeting in New Delhi
items.summary: NEW DELHI, May 15 — Malaysia has reiterated its position that all disputes in West Asia must be resolved through dialogue, diplomacy and respect for international law, says Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. In a Facebook post today, he said the position was conveyed during a meeting with Iranian Foreign Minister Dr Seyed Abbas Araghchi on the sidelines of the 2026 BRICS Partners Foreign Ministers’ Meeting here yesterday. At the meeting, Mohamad also held bilateral discussions with Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Eduardo Rodríguez Parrilla.

items.title: Search continues on Day 5 for missing five after migrant boat sinks near Pulau Pangkor, death toll now nine
items.summary: IPOH, May 15 — Another body of a victim from the boat carrying Indonesian illegal immigrants that sank in the waters off Pulau Pangkor was found this evening, bringing the death toll so far to nine. The Perak branch of the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA), in a statement, said the male victim’s body was discovered by Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) asset, KD Sri Indera Sakti. “The ninth body was found at a position 4.5 nautical miles southwest of Pulau Pangkor at about 5.31pm yesterday.

items.title: Sultan Nazrin honours teachers with special Friday sermon role across Perak mosques
items.summary: IPOH, May 15 — The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, has consented to 625 educators and teachers in the state delivering Friday sermons today in conjunction with Teachers’ Day, which is celebrated annually on May 16. “His Royal Highness the Sultan of Perak Darul Ridzuan, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah ibni Almarhum Sultan Azlan Muhibbuddin Shah Al-Maghfur-Lah, has decreed that the Friday sermon in Perak on May 15, 2026, should carry the theme of teachers, in conjunction with Teachers’ Day on May 16, 2026. Mohd Annuar said following the royal consent, Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad would lead educators and teachers in delivering the Friday sermon at the State Mosque (Sultan Idris Shah II Mosque).

items.title: Babies safely returned after accidental mix-up at KL hospital ward
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — An incident involving the accidental swapping of babies at a ward in Tunku Azizah Hospital (HTA) has been resolved, with the babies safely returned to their respective mothers. HTA director Dr Shamsul Anuar Kamarudin said immediate corrective action was taken once the incident was discovered. He said the hospital had provided the necessary explanation and support to the families involved, adding that preliminary checks found no breastfeeding or medical procedures that compromised the babies’ safety during the incident.

items.title: Environment Dept cites national security in restricting EIA report downloads
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 15 — Full Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) reports are not made publicly available for download to safeguard ownership and copyright protection, information security, and national security interests, the Department of Environment (DOE) said in a statement. It said EIA reports usually contain detailed technical information, including data on strategic locations, critical infrastructure, development plans, site coordinates and other sensitive material that require controlled access. The DOE added that access to the Executive Summary of EIA reports for public reference is available through designated platforms based on submitted applications.

items.title: Grab engineer Mohammad Hasif Afiq becomes first Malaysian in Apple’s global developer honours programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Software engineer Mohammad Hasif Afiq has made history as the first Malaysian to be featured in Apple’s newly introduced Developer Recognition programme. Hasif described the recognition from the global tech giant as a surreal and emotional moment, after being notified via an official email from Apple’s Developer Relations team yesterday. Hasif said that over the years, he has developed several applications, including Amalan Mukmin, Vacay, Hidayah App, Lylyn App and Bantu App, with some gaining strong traction locally, especially the Bantu App during the COVID-19 pandemic.

items.title: Knife found beside dead technician in Johor Bahru apartment, police suspect murder
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 15 — A male technician was found dead, believed to have been murdered, in an apartment here Wednesday. Johor Bahru Selatan district police chief ACP Raub Selamat said his team received a report regarding the incident at around 11.12 pm. He said the initial investigation found that the victim was 33 years old.

items.title: ‘Mushroom-flavoured’ vape liquid dispute ends in fatal stabbing at Melaka playground, four suspects in custody
items.summary: JASIN, May 15 — A 30-year-old man died after being stabbed during a dispute over ‘mushroom-flavoured’ vape liquid at a playground in Merlimau last night, police said. Jasin police chief Supt Lee Robert said the victim suffered a stab wound to the right side of his back that pierced his lung, as well as bleeding to the head. “The victim died while receiving treatment at Melaka Hospital at 1.26 pm today.

items.title: Kopitiam nostalgia goes mainstream as Malaysia’s cafe culture turns back the clock
items.summary: Inside, customers linger over kopi and kaya toast, some chatting in groups while others sit alone with laptops open — scenes once closely associated with international coffee chains. Across urban shopping malls, kopitiam -style chains such as Oriental Kopi, Nanyang Cafe and Hock Kee Heritage Kopitiam are repackaging an imagined version of old Malaya into a contemporary café experience built around familiarity and nostalgia. Genevieve Lim, 28, said modern kopitiam chains combine local food with the practical comforts younger consumers increasingly expect from contemporary cafés.

items.title: MACC confirms getting USB drive, documents from Rafizi for ARM deal probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 14 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) confirmed it has received several documents and video recordings from former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli to assist in investigations into alleged abuse of power and misappropriation involving government contracts worth nearly RM1.1 billion. MACC Senior Director of the Investigation Division said Rafizi arrived with five lawyers and handed over printed documents and video recordings in a USB flash drive, believed to be related to ARM Limited UK. Mohd Hafaz said the MACC also recorded additional statements from Rafizi, and the process ended at 1.30 pm.
